name: Mongolian Plateau
description: High plateau of Mongolia, heartland of successive steppe empires

periods.note: Xiongnu Empire — first nomadic empire from the Mongolian steppe
Xianbei → Rouran — successive nomadic confederations; Xianbei later founded Northern Wei in China
Göktürk Khaganate — Mongolian plateau as core of Turkic empire
Uyghur Khaganate — replaced Göktürks on the plateau
Khitan Liao Dynasty — Mongolic-speaking confederation; also ruled northern China

ethnic_composition.presence: dominant; heartland of the world's largest contiguous empire

periods.note: Northern Yuan / various Mongol khanates — post-imperial fragmentation

ethnic_composition.note: Qing used marriage alliances and Buddhism to bind Mongols; Inner Mongolia settled by Han later

periods.note: Bogd Khanate — theocratic Mongolian state under the 8th Jebtsundamba Khutughtu (the 'Living Buddha') after declaring independence from Qing; Soviet-backed revolution ended it
Mongolian People's Republic — Soviet satellite; destruction of Buddhism (700 monasteries); collectivisation of herds; Soviet military base against Japan and China; transition to democracy 1990

ethnic_composition.presence: Soviet advisors and small permanent community

periods.note: Republic of Mongolia — democratic since 1992; one of the world's few successful post-communist democratic transitions; economy dependent on mining (copper, coal, gold); caught between Russia and China; significant nomadic herding tradition persists alongside rapid urbanisation of Ulaanbaatar
